interpretations.title: Results and Interpretations
rows: Vein does not collapse when pressed
No color or sound in a vein

faqs.question: Is the scan painful?
faqs.answer: No. The probe only touches your skin; you may feel slight pressure.

faqs.question: How long does the test take?
Can I drive after the scan?
Do I need to stop medications before the test?
faqs.answer: No. Continue all prescribed drugs unless your doctor says otherwise.

faqs.question: When will I receive my results?
faqs.answer: Same day for routine scans; urgent cases within 2 hours.

seo.title: Venous Doppler Study Bilateral Lower Limb | Cadabams Diagnostics
seo.description: Venous Doppler Ultrasound for Bilateral Lower Limbs. Detect DVT and varicose veins painlessly in 15-30 mins. Accurate, non-invasive assessment.

test.markdown:
## Venous Doppler Study of Bilateral Lower Limb Ultrasound Scan Overview

- \*\*What it checks:\*\* Blood flow speed, vein compressibility, valve function, and clot presence in both legs
- \*\*Why choose it:\*\* 100 % non-invasive, zero radiation, and gives real-time images in under 30 minutes
---
## What is Venous Doppler Study of Bilateral Lower Limb Ultrasound Scan?
### Definition and Purpose
A \*\*DVT ultrasound scan\*\* (another name for this test) uses high-frequency sound waves to create live pictures of the deep and superficial veins in each leg. Its main job is to confirm or rule out \*\*deep vein thrombosis (DVT)\*\* and other venous disorders.
### How Doppler Ultrasound Technology Works
A hand-held probe sends sound into the leg. Moving blood cells bounce those sounds back at different speeds, which the machine turns into color maps and graphs. Your technician can “hear” and “see” whether blood is flowing smoothly or meeting an obstruction.
---
## Types of Venous Doppler Study of Bilateral Lower Limb Ultrasound Scan
| Type | What It Adds |
|--------------|----------------------------------------|
| \*\*Color Doppler\*\* | Shows direction and speed of blood in color |
| \*\*Spectral Doppler\*\* | Displays blood-flow waveforms for precise velocity |
| \*\*Power Doppler\*\* | Highlights small, slow-flow vessels in deeper tissue |
---

## When and Who Needs to Take a Venous Doppler Study of Bilateral Lower Limb Ultrasound Scan?

### Symptoms That Prompt the Test
- Sudden leg pain or cramping
- Swelling in one or both calves
- Warm, red, or discolored skin
- Visible varicose or bulging veins
### Common Risk Factors
- Recent surgery or long-haul travel
- Pregnancy or post-partum period
- Obesity, smoking, or a family history of clots
- Hormone therapy or cancer treatment
---

## Risks & Limitations of the Venous Doppler Study of Bilateral Lower Limb Ultrasound Scan

- \*\*Minimal Risks:\*\* No radiation, no contrast dye, safe for all ages
- \*\*Limiting Factors:\*\* Severe leg swelling, open wounds, or heavy leg creams can reduce image clarity
---

## List of Parameters Considered During the Venous Doppler Study of Bilateral Lower Limb Ultrasound Scan

- \*\*Blood flow velocity\*\* – faster or slower than normal?
- \*\*Vein compressibility\*\* – does the vein flatten when pressed?
- \*\*Valve function\*\* – do valves close fully to stop back-flow?
- \*\*Presence of clots\*\* – any solid mass blocking the vein?
---

## Reasons for Taking a Venous Doppler Study of Bilateral Lower Limb Ultrasound Scan

- \*\*Suspected DVT\*\* – to prevent pulmonary embolism
- \*\*Chronic venous insufficiency\*\* – tired, heavy legs
- \*\*Varicose veins\*\* – before planning treatment
- \*\*Unexplained leg swelling or pain\*\* – one or both limbs
---

## Benefits of Taking the Venous Doppler Study of Bilateral Lower Limb Ultrasound Scan

- \*\*Non-invasive & painless\*\* – no needles, no incisions
- \*\*No radiation\*\* – safe for pregnant women when clinically indicated
- \*\*Real-time imaging\*\* – see problems instantly
- \*\*Early detection\*\* – clots caught before complications arise
---

## Illnesses Diagnosed with Venous Doppler Study of Bilateral Lower Limb Ultrasound Scan

- \*\*Deep vein thrombosis (DVT)\*\*
- \*\*Superficial thrombophlebitis\*\*
- \*\*Venous reflux disease\*\*
- \*\*Chronic venous hypertension\*\*
---

## Preparing for Venous Doppler Study of Bilateral Lower Limb Ultrasound Scan

- Wear \*\*loose-fitting pants or shorts\*\*
- Remove \*\*compression stockings\*\* before arrival
- \*\*No fasting\*\* – eat and hydrate normally
---
## Pre-requisites for Venous Doppler Study of Bilateral Lower Limb Ultrasound Scan
- A \*\*doctor’s referral\*\* (physical or digital)
- Brief \*\*medical history\*\* of symptoms and onset
- \*\*Current medication list\*\* – especially blood thinners
---
## Best Time to Take the Venous Doppler Study of Bilateral Lower Limb Ultrasound Scan
- \*\*At first sign of symptoms\*\* – don’t wait for worsening pain
- \*\*Post-treatment follow-up\*\* – to confirm clots have dissolved
- \*\*Routine screening\*\* – for high-risk or post-surgical patients
---
## Eligibility
- \*\*Adults\*\* (18 +) with any leg discomfort or swelling
- \*\*Post-surgical patients\*\* – knee, hip, or cancer surgery within 90 days
- \*\*Pregnant women\*\* – only if prescribed by a qualified doctor
---
## Procedure for Taking a Venous Doppler Study of Bilateral Lower Limb Ultrasound Scan
1. \*\*Positioning\*\* – lie on a comfortable couch, legs slightly turned out
2. \*\*Gel Application\*\* – warm, water-based gel on the groin, thigh, and calf
3. \*\*Probe Movement\*\* – technician glides a small handheld device along each vein
4. \*\*Duration\*\* – 15–30 minutes total, both legs scanned
---
## Caution Before Taking the Test
- Inform staff of \*\*open wounds or skin infections\*\*
- Avoid \*\*heavy moisturizers or oils\*\* on legs on test day
- Drink water – \*\*well-hydrated veins\*\* are easier to image
---
